speaker: scarlett
text: Sir, you are no gentleman!

speaker: rhett
text: And you, miss, are no lady. Don't think that I hold that against you. Ladies have never held any charm for me.

speaker: scarlett
text: How dare you say such things to me! You don't even know me!

speaker: rhett
text: I know you. I watched you throwing yourself at Ashley Wilkes today. It was quite a spectacle.

speaker: scarlett
text: You are the most ill-mannered man I have ever met.

speaker: rhett
text: And you are the most honest woman I've ever seen. When you're not trying to pretend otherwise.

speaker: scarlett
text: I love Ashley. I've always loved him and I always will.

speaker: rhett
text: You love Ashley Wilkes? The way a child loves a shiny toy. He's everything you think a gentleman should be, but he'll never make you happy.

speaker: scarlett
text: What do you know about love or happiness?

speaker: rhett
text: I know that what you feel for Ashley is an illusion. You love the idea of him, not the man himself.

speaker: scarlett
text: I'll think about that tomorrow. Right now I have more important things to worry about.

speaker: rhett
text: That's your solution for everything, isn't it? Tomorrow. You'll think about it tomorrow.

speaker: scarlett
text: I need money. Tara needs money. The taxes alone could ruin us. I'll do whatever it takes.

speaker: rhett
text: Whatever it takes? Be careful with that kind of determination, Scarlett. It has a way of costing more than you expect.

speaker: scarlett
text: You wouldn't understand. You've never gone hungry. You've never watched everything you love crumble.

speaker: rhett
text: Haven't I? I left Charleston with nothing but the clothes on my back and my wits. The difference is I don't pretend it didn't happen.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett, I need your help. I need three hundred dollars to pay the taxes on Tara.

speaker: rhett
text: You came here looking like this, dressed up to seduce me into giving you money. I must say, you almost had me.

speaker: scarlett
text: I don't know what you mean. I just wanted to look nice for you.

speaker: rhett
text: Scarlett, I've always admired your spirit, but I've always seen through you too. Your hands tell the real story — those aren't the hands of a lady of leisure.

speaker: scarlett
text: As God is my witness, I'll never be hungry again. Never.

speaker: scarlett
text: I married Charles Hamilton to hurt Ashley. I married Frank Kennedy for his money. Does that shock you?

speaker: rhett
text: Nothing you do shocks me, Scarlett. That's why we're alike. We're both selfish and shrewd, and we both know what we want.

speaker: scarlett
text: I am not selfish! Everything I've done has been for Tara and my family.

speaker: rhett
text: Keep telling yourself that. You did it for Tara, yes. But mostly you did it because you can't stand to lose. At anything.

speaker: scarlett
text: And what's wrong with not wanting to lose?

speaker: rhett
text: Nothing at all. It's one of the things I love about you. Marry me, Scarlett.

speaker: scarlett
text: I don't love you, Rhett. And I don't want to marry someone I don't love.

speaker: rhett
text: You don't love Ashley either, not really. At least with me you'd have fun. And all the money and pretty dresses you could ever want.

speaker: scarlett
text: You make marriage sound like a business transaction.

speaker: rhett
text: All marriages are business transactions. Ours would just be more honest about it.

speaker: scarlett
text: Fine. I'll marry you. But don't say I didn't warn you.

speaker: rhett
text: I never expected anything less than a warning from you, my dear.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett, buy me that hat. And that dress. And those earrings. Oh, everything!

speaker: rhett
text: If buying things makes you happy, then by all means. Though I suspect nothing will ever quite fill that hole.

speaker: scarlett
text: What hole? I'm perfectly happy. I have everything I ever wanted.

speaker: rhett
text: Everything except Ashley Wilkes.

speaker: scarlett
text: Why must you always bring up Ashley? Are you jealous?

speaker: rhett
text: Jealous? Of that pale, dreaming boy? No. I'm merely observing that you keep reaching for something you can never have, while ignoring what's right in front of you.

speaker: scarlett
text: I don't need lectures from you. I need you to stop treating our marriage like a joke.

speaker: rhett
text: A joke? I've given you everything, Scarlett. A house, money, status. The only thing I haven't given you is Ashley, and that's the only thing you seem to want.

speaker: scarlett
text: That's not true and you know it. I care about this family. I care about Bonnie.

speaker: rhett
text: Bonnie is the only honest thing between us. She's the only person you love without an agenda.

speaker: scarlett
text: You're cruel, Rhett. You say these things to hurt me.

speaker: rhett
text: I say these things because someone has to tell you the truth. Everyone else is too afraid of you or too in love with the idea of you.

speaker: scarlett
text: Mammy isn't afraid of me. Mammy tells me the truth.

speaker: rhett
text: Mammy is the wisest person in this house. She always has been. I respect her more than anyone in Atlanta.

speaker: scarlett
text: I want to throw the biggest party Atlanta has ever seen. I want everyone to see our house.

speaker: rhett
text: You want them to see how far you've come from that dirt farm. You want to rub their noses in your success.

speaker: scarlett
text: Is that so terrible? They all looked down on me. The old guard, the Meades, the Merriweathers — they judged me.

speaker: rhett
text: They judged us both. The difference is I never cared what they thought. You still do, and it eats you alive.

speaker: scarlett
text: I don't care what they think! I just want to show them—

speaker: rhett
text: That's exactly what caring looks like, darling.

speaker: scarlett
text: The lumber business is doing well. I'm making more money than most men in Atlanta.

speaker: rhett
text: And working your convict laborers half to death in the process. Even I have scruples about that.

speaker: scarlett
text: They're criminals! And I pay them. It's just good business.

speaker: rhett
text: There's the Scarlett I know. Practical to the bone, even when it costs her her soul.

speaker: scarlett
text: My soul is just fine, thank you. My soul survived the war, survived hunger, survived losing everything.

speaker: rhett
text: Survived, yes. But at what cost? You've become exactly the kind of person you used to despise.

speaker: scarlett
text: I had to become this person. No one was going to save me or Tara. I saved myself.

speaker: rhett
text: And I admire that about you. Truly. But there's a difference between surviving and living, and I'm not sure you know it anymore.

speaker: scarlett
text: Bonnie is the most beautiful child in the world. She has your dark hair and my green eyes.

speaker: rhett
text: She's the best thing I've ever done. The only truly good thing. She'll have everything — love, education, acceptance. Everything I didn't have.

speaker: scarlett
text: You spoil her terribly. She needs discipline, not another pony.

speaker: rhett
text: She needs to know she's loved without conditions. That's something neither of us ever had, Scarlett.

speaker: scarlett
text: My father loved me. Pa loved me more than anything.

speaker: rhett
text: Gerald loved you, yes. But he also loved Tara and his whiskey and his horses. You were always competing for attention, even with the land itself.

speaker: scarlett
text: Don't you dare speak ill of my father.

speaker: rhett
text: I'm not speaking ill of him. I understand him perfectly. I'm the same way — I love Bonnie, but I also love my freedom and my whiskey and my gambling. The difference is I know it.

speaker: scarlett
text: Sometimes I dream about Tara. About the red earth and the cotton fields. Before the war, when everything made sense.

speaker: rhett
text: Nothing made sense before the war, Scarlett. You just didn't know enough to see the cracks.

speaker: scarlett
text: I was happy then. I was the belle of the county. Every boy wanted to dance with me.

speaker: rhett
text: You were sixteen and vain and beautiful and completely unaware of how the world worked. That's not happiness, that's ignorance.

speaker: scarlett
text: Well, ignorance was bliss. Now I know how the world works and I'm miserable.

speaker: rhett
text: You're miserable because you keep wanting what you can't have and refusing what's offered to you.

speaker: scarlett
text: Melanie is the only truly good person I know. I hate her for it sometimes.

speaker: rhett
text: You don't hate Melanie. You're terrified of her because she sees the good in you that you try so hard to hide.

speaker: scarlett
text: I don't hide anything. Everyone thinks I'm selfish and shallow.

speaker: rhett
text: Because you want them to. It's safer than letting them see you're afraid. I've done the same thing my whole life — play the scoundrel so no one expects anything real from you.

speaker: scarlett
text: You really think we're alike?

speaker: rhett
text: Two sides of the same coin. Both fighters, both survivors, both too proud to admit when we're hurting.

speaker: scarlett
text: I went to see Ashley today. I needed someone to talk to.

speaker: rhett
text: Of course you did. Whenever things get hard between us, you run to your fantasy of Ashley.

speaker: scarlett
text: He understands me in ways you never will.

speaker: rhett
text: He doesn't understand you at all. He loves an image of Southern womanhood that you've never been. I love who you actually are, and it's still not enough.

speaker: scarlett
text: Don't say that. Don't say you love me.

speaker: rhett
text: Why not? Because it means you'd have to make a choice? Because if I love you and Ashley loves Melanie, then there's no one left for you to blame?

speaker: scarlett
text: After Bonnie died, I felt something break inside Rhett. Something I couldn't fix.

speaker: rhett
text: When Bonnie died, she took everything good in me with her. She was my hope that something pure could come from two damaged people.

speaker: scarlett
text: We still have each other. Doesn't that count for something?

speaker: rhett
text: I used to think so. I spent years pouring love into you, Scarlett, waiting for you to notice. But you can't pour love into a cup that's always turned upside down.

speaker: scarlett
text: I didn't know, Rhett. I didn't know how much you loved me. I was blind.

speaker: rhett
text: Yes, you were. And I was a fool for thinking I could love enough for both of us.

speaker: scarlett
text: Rhett! Rhett, don't go! Where will I go? What will I do?

speaker: rhett
text: Frankly, my dear, I don't give a damn.

speaker: scarlett
text: I can't think about this now. I'll think about it tomorrow... After all, tomorrow is another day.

speaker: scarlett
text: I lied to myself about Ashley for years. I see that now. The truth was always Rhett.

speaker: scarlett
text: If I have to lie, steal, cheat, or kill — I'll never be hungry again.

speaker: rhett
text: There's a soldier in you, Scarlett. Most women would have crumbled. You picked up a gun and fought.

speaker: scarlett
text: I didn't have a choice. Someone had to take charge. The men were all dead or useless.

speaker: rhett
text: You always had a choice. You just always chose to fight. It's magnificent and terrible at the same time.

speaker: scarlett
text: I pulled Melanie through childbirth with the Yankees burning Atlanta around us. Don't tell me I don't care about people.

speaker: rhett
text: I know you care. You just care the way a general cares about soldiers — fiercely, but from a distance.

speaker: scarlett
text: Money is the most important thing in the world. Without it, you're nothing.

speaker: rhett
text: Money is a tool, Scarlett. You've made it your god. There's a difference.

speaker: scarlett
text: Easy for you to say. You've always had money. You don't know what it's like to starve.

speaker: rhett
text: I made my money running blockades while boys were dying. I'm not exactly a saint. But I never confused my bank account with my worth as a person.

speaker: scarlett
text: The old South is dead. Good riddance. I'd rather build something new than cry over what we lost.

speaker: rhett
text: Now that — that right there — is why I fell in love with you. Everyone else in this town is mourning a world that deserved to die, and you're already building the next one.

speaker: scarlett
text: I don't understand why people cling to the past. The past didn't feed my children.

speaker: rhett
text: Ashley clings to the past. That's why he's drowning and you're swimming. You two were never meant for each other.
